Brightness transition duration.description: If you would like your lights brightness transitions to not be instantaneous you can set a specific speed in seconds here. The default is 0.

Days between updating certificates.description: During pairing Matter depends on certicates that validate official Matter devices. Whenever you start a pairing process, and a few days have passed, then this addon will try to download the latest certificates from csa-iot.org. What's the minimum amount of days before an update should be attempted? The default is 14 days. Set it to zero to fully disable updating (which means you will unable to pair newly released Matter devices).

Do not use Hotspot as WiFi network for d.description: Advanced. The Hotspot offers a separate guest WiFi network for your smart devices. Using it will give you more insight into the communication habits of your Matter devices, and you can also block connections you don't want or trust. The default is to use the Hotspot feature to offer more protection against corporate surveillance.

Vendor ID.description: Advanced. You can manually set the Vendor ID of the Matter fabric. If you change this you will have to pair all your devices again. For example, the Home Assistant vendor ID is 134B

Thread channel.description: Advanced. Which wireless channel the Thread network should use. Common recommendations are 11, 15, 20 and 25 or 26. Fun fact: Apple always uses 26. The default is 26.

Matter server type.description: Advanced. You can choose which Matter server to run, the old but stable Python version, or the new but barely tested Node JS version (which also uses more memory). The default is Python.

Enable Matter server dashboard.description: Advanced. The Matter Server comes with it's own little webserver (on port 5580) that offers details about the Matter network. This feature is disabled by default, but you can enable it here.

Debugging.description: Advanced. Will generate details about the addon's processes in the internal log.
